deps_note: package.json has ZERO observability SDKs — no Sentry, OpenTelemetry, Datadog, Logtail, pino, winston, or any structured-logging library. Only framer-motion, lucide-react, next, next-themes, react, recharts, sonner (UI toast only), zod, zustand. Observability is entirely stdout-based (console.log/error/warn) and relies on whatever platform (Railway) aggregates them.

samples.verdict: real structured logging — parseable by Vercel Logs / log aggregators
unstructured — fine for eyeballing, useless for querying
unstructured; error object serialization depends on Node default (often loses stack)

logging.overall: ONE cron endpoint emits structured JSON. Everything else in the pipeline (github-adapter, ingest, normalizer, backfill, stargazer-backfill, social-adapters, nitter-adapter, file-persistence, singleton) is free-form console.* with bracketed tags like [github-adapter], [ingest], [cron:ingest]. Grep-friendly but not query-friendly.

error_handling.detail: 429 and 5xx retried up to 3 total attempts with exp backoff (1s, 2s). Network errors retried. After exhaustion: console.error + return null. 403 is NOT retried (correct — 403 with remaining=0 is rate limit handled via isRateLimited). 404 returned to caller. Reads x-ratelimit-remaining / x-ratelimit-reset on every response — good.
Non-2xx and parse errors: console.error then return null/0. Caller sees degraded data but no exception. No Sentry/alert — errors only surface if an operator reads the Railway log stream.

Wraps entire per-repo ingest in try/catch. Never throws. Failures recorded in IngestResult.error + ok=false and counted as `failed` in batch result. This is the one place error rollup happens — visible via /api/cron/ingest response body, not stored anywhere durable.
`if (!res.ok) break` — a 403/429/5xx on the Events API silently truncates the backfill. No log. No counter. The backfill-top cron will report success with partial data.
Outer catch emits structured JSON with status:error + message, returns HTTP 500. Good.
console.error and continue. No aggregation, no alerting.

error_handling.site: assorted API routes (persist, cleanup, backfill-history, rebuild, alerts/rules, sidebar-data)
error_handling.detail: Grep found 18 bare catches. Several in API routes silently swallow errors and return degraded data or fall through. Examples: src/app/api/pipeline/persist/route.ts:49, src/app/api/pipeline/cleanup/route.ts:46,132, src/app/api/pipeline/sidebar-data/route.ts:81, src/app/api/pipeline/rebuild/route.ts:96, src/app/api/pipeline/backfill-history/route.ts:71. Silent failure risk.

health_endpoint.note: No /api/health or /api/healthz. The closest thing is GET /api/pipeline/status.

asserts.note: A stale seed from 3 weeks ago still returns seeded:true — does NOT assert freshness.
Counts, not freshness. A frozen system returns non-zero counts.

asserts.source: max(score.computedAt) across scoreStore; falls back to newest snapshot.capturedAt
asserts.note: REAL. Pulled from actual data. Operator could compare to `now()` to detect stuck pipeline — but the endpoint does NOT do that comparison or turn red when stale.
REAL. But only surfaces current budget, not whether the cron actually ran.

does_not_assert: DB / filesystem reachability (no probe on disk-persistence layer)
lastRefreshAt within N seconds threshold (no freshness gate)
cron last-run-status (no record of cron invocations)
mock adapter is NOT active (guard lives only inside createGitHubAdapter at call time)
HTTP status always 200 unless the handler itself throws — never returns 503/degraded based on data state
health_endpoint.verdict: status endpoint is a data-counts snapshot, not a health check. Will return 200 OK indefinitely on a dead pipeline.

admin_dashboard.note: No /admin, /status, /dashboard route exists under src/app/. Operator-facing observability is: (a) the public StatsBar on the homepage, (b) the JSON at /api/pipeline/status, (c) Railway/Vercel log tails.

fields.label: Repos tracked (StatsBar)
fields.evidence: src/components/terminal/StatsBar.tsx:37 → stats.totalRepos from getGlobalStats() which iterates repoStore.getActive()

fields.label: Hot count (StatsBar)
fields.evidence: getGlobalStats() counts movementStatus==='hot' at query time

fields.label: Last refresh (StatsBar)
fields.evidence: getGlobalStats(): max(score.computedAt) across scoreStore, fallback to snapshot.capturedAt. NOT mount time. NOT build time. NOT Date.now(). Confirmed by reading aggregate.ts:111-127.

cron_failure_visibility: ZERO active signal. No Sentry/webhook/email. If Vercel Cron fails: Vercel logs it, nobody is notified. BUT vercel.json contains only redirects — no `crons` block — so Vercel Cron is NOT configured here. The app runs on Railway (redirect target starscreener-production.up.railway.app) and no railway.toml is present. Cron mechanism is external/undocumented in repo; operator must know to check Railway scheduler dashboard manually. The one mitigation: successful cron runs emit structured JSON lines that Railway/Vercel logs aggregate, so a tail `| jq 'select(.status!="ok")'` would surface failures — if someone runs it. No automated alert, no delta-against-last-run tracking.
rate_limit_surface_detail: github-adapter.ts updates rateLimit state from x-ratelimit-remaining / x-ratelimit-reset on every response (line 236-251), logs per-request `rl=N` (line 214), and short-circuits further requests when remaining<=0 until reset (line 253-258). Surfaced on /api/pipeline/status as rateLimitRemaining and on /api/cron/ingest response body. NOT surfaced in the user-facing StatsBar.

no_mock_runtime_guard.detail: createGitHubAdapter throws at construction time if useMock=true without STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK=true, AND throws if no GITHUB_TOKEN is set (even with useMock=false). Both cron/ingest and seed.ts explicitly pass useMock:false. Strong guard — mock cannot silently activate in prod.
no_mock_runtime_guard.gap: Guard runs ONLY when createGitHubAdapter is called (per-cron-run). No startup/boot-time assertion. Nothing in /api/pipeline/status reveals adapter.id==='github' to a caller — the ingest endpoint returns `source: "github"` but the status endpoint does not. An operator cannot tell from /api/pipeline/status whether the most recent ingest used mock or real.

vibes_bugs.where: /api/pipeline/status always returns HTTP 200 regardless of data age
vibes_bugs.drift_risk: Operator hitting the URL sees JSON with lastRefreshAt: '2026-03-01' and rateLimitRemaining: 4800, everything looks green. No red light. A health checker pinging this endpoint reports UP even if ingestion has been dead for a month.

vibes_bugs.where: StatsBar 'Last refresh: 2 hours ago' on the landing page is the only visible freshness signal to users
vibes_bugs.drift_risk: Copy is relative-time only; no color/state turns red when stale. If ingestion stops, users see the number drift from '2h' to '4d' to '3w' with no visual alert. Freshness threshold is undefined.

vibes_bugs.where: events-backfill silent `if (!res.ok) break` on the Events API
vibes_bugs.drift_risk: Mega-repo daily star counts will silently truncate on 422/403 from GitHub. The surrounding cron reports success; the data just becomes partial with no counter incremented.
Cleanup, persist, rebuild, sidebar-data, backfill-history routes can throw internally and return a degraded response without any log line. Bugs become invisible.

A fatal error in the cron handler emits one structured JSON line to stdout and is forgotten. Unless the operator is watching the log stream at that moment, or runs a periodic `jq 'select(.status=="error")'` query, the error is lost.

vibes_bugs.where: No cron config in repo (vercel.json=redirects only, no railway.toml)
vibes_bugs.drift_risk: Cron schedule lives entirely in the Railway dashboard. A paused/deleted scheduler is invisible to anyone reading the repo. No on-call alert if the cron never fires.

If STARSCREENER_ALLOW_MOCK slips through on a non-prod env that a stakeholder is reviewing, the status endpoint won't reveal it. (Note: the runtime guard prevents silent activation on the ingest path, so actual prod drift risk is low.)

vibes_bugs.where: sonner toast is the ONLY alerting surface
vibes_bugs.drift_risk: UI-level only. No operator notification pipeline.

grade_justification: Not F because: (1) structured JSON logging exists on the critical cron path; (2) GitHub rate-limit tracking is real (reads X-RateLimit-Remaining/Reset, retries 429/5xx with exp backoff); (3) the no-mock runtime guard actually throws in createGitHubAdapter; (4) lastRefreshAt is computed from real store data, not hardcoded/mount time; (5) StatsBar dashboard fields are wired to real counters. Not C or better because: (1) zero observability SDK — no Sentry, OTel, or any alerting substrate; (2) /api/pipeline/status is a counts snapshot, not a real health check — it returns 200 on a frozen pipeline; (3) no /api/health endpoint; (4) no admin/status UI page; (5) 18 bare `} catch {}` silent-swallow sites; (6) events-backfill silently truncates on HTTP errors; (7) cron failure has ZERO active notification — an operator only finds out if they happen to tail the log; (8) no cron config in vercel.json or railway.toml, so the schedule is invisible to the repo; (9) the user-facing 'Last refresh' has no stale threshold or color state. Net: an operator CANNOT tell if ingestion is broken right now without manually tailing Railway logs or eyeballing a relative timestamp.
